root.title: Cúram 8.1.2 What's New Guide
root.summary: Front matter and legal pages for the Cúram 8.1.2 What’s New Guide, covering editions for Cúram 8.1, 8.1.1, and 8.1.2. It includes the table of contents, a release overview, and the main “What’s new” section summarizing updates across these versions. Key topics include CSRF-protected web navigation docs, enhanced text translation, external application framework improvements, IEG script guidance, new financial hook points, AppResource documentation, product rebranding to Cúram by Merative, accessibility improvements, dropdown and sortable-column UX enhancements, Person Evidence Management, context panel refresh guidance, Azure SSO/OIDC setup, AKS support for development/test, and deprecated feature removals. It also contains a brief release-notes pointer advising review before installation/configuration, plus full notices, privacy policy, and trademarks/legal terms.

children.title: Note
children.summary: Brief front-matter note directing readers to review the Notices section before using the information or the product. Includes Merative US L.P. copyright notice for 2012 and 2024.

children.title: Edition
Edition — pages 5-14
children.summary: Front matter for the Cúram 8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2 documentation, including edition/copyright and the table of contents. It introduces the “What’s new” sections for 8.1.2, 8.1.1, and 8.1, plus release notes and notices. The “What’s new” content highlights major changes such as CSRF navigation docs, text translation support, external app framework updates, IEG script guidance, a new financial hook point, product rebranding to Cúram by Merative, accessibility improvements, dropdown UX enhancements, Person Evidence Management, new JMX properties, pre-authorization sharing, OSS updates, and AKS support.

children.title: Edition — pages 15-20
children.summary: This section covers the Cúram 8.1.2 “What’s new” and release notes. It highlights UI improvements like dropdown matching and sortable column icons, enhanced language-specific text translation, and updated documentation for refreshing the context panel. It also introduces new financial hook points, expanded SSO guidance for Microsoft Azure (SAML and OIDC), AKS support for dev/test environments, and removal of deprecated features. The final pages begin the release notes section, noting they list changes and fixed defects per release.

Front matter for Cúram 8.1/8.1.1/8.1.2, including edition/copyright and the table of contents, followed by the start of “What’s new” and release notes. Covers 8.1.2 updates such as CSRF navigation docs, text translation, external app framework, IEG guidance, a new financial hook point, Azure/SSO and AKS support, and OSS updates; 8.1.1 rebranding, accessibility, dropdown UX, Person Evidence Management, JMX properties, pre-authorization sharing, and OSS updates; and 8.1 dropdown/sort improvements, context panel refresh guidance, translation, financial hook points, Azure SSO, AKS, and deprecated feature removals.

children.title: Notices
Privacy policy
children.summary: This section covers legal notices: sample data disclaimer, copyright license for sample source programs, warranty/liability limits, the Merative privacy policy link, and trademark ownership statements for Merative, IBM, Adobe, Oracle, Linux, Microsoft, UNIX, and others. It also notes that sample names are fictitious and gives the copyright notice © Merative US L.P. 2012, 2024.

children.title: Trademarks
children.summary: This section lists trademark and copyright notices for Merative and third-party products, including IBM, Adobe, Oracle, Linux, Microsoft, and UNIX. It also includes a privacy policy link, a sample-code copyright license and warranty disclaimer, and a note that example names in the document are fictitious. Useful for legal, attribution, and usage-rights information.

Legal notices and usage terms for the publication: personal and enterprise use are allowed only if proprietary notices are preserved, with restrictions on redistribution and derivative works. It states no additional rights are granted, Merative may withdraw permission, and export laws apply. The content is provided “as-is” with no warranties, and Merative does not license patents. It also disclaims responsibility for non-Merative products/sites and includes the © Merative US L.P. 2012, 2024 notice.

children.title: Contents
children.summary: This is the table of contents for the document. It lists front matter and major sections, including Note, Edition, “What’s new” (with subsections for Cúram 8.1.2, 8.1.1, and 8.1), Release notes, Notices, Privacy policy, and Trademarks, along with their page numbers. Useful for navigating the guide rather than substantive content.

children.title: 1 What's new
1.1 What's new in Cúram 8.1.2
children.summary: Cúram 8.1.2 (released 25 April 2024) introduces updates for all users, caseworkers, and developers. Key changes include CSRF-protected web page navigation documentation, localized eligibility/product delivery results when switching language, expanded Cúram Web Design System support for external users and authorized representatives, IEG scripting guidance and sample patterns, and a new financial hook point for payment offset logic. It also points to related release notes and guides for implementation details.

children.title: 1.2 What's new in Cúram 8.1.1
children.summary: Cúram 8.1.1 (released 8 Dec 2023) introduces rebranding to Cúram by Merative, broad accessibility improvements, better dropdown filtering/sorting for caseworkers, and new Person Evidence Management (separately licensed) for centralized person-level evidence handling. For administrators, it adds new JMX application properties for batch statistics. For developers, it adds pre-authorization sharing and updates over 40 open-source libraries.

children.title: 1.3 What's new in Cúram 8.1
children.summary: Cúram 8.1 (released 22 June 2023) highlights changes for caseworkers, administrators, developers, security, and removed features. Key updates include improved combo-box dropdown search/filter behavior and new sortable column icons for caseworkers; enhanced text translation support and clearer context panel refresh configuration for administrators; new financial hook points and AKS support in dev/test for developers; and expanded SSO guidance for Microsoft Azure with SAML and OIDC. Deprecated features were removed.

Overview of Cúram “What’s new” for versions 8.1.2, 8.1.1, and 8.1. Key topics include CSRF-protected web navigation docs, localized eligibility/display results, expanded Cúram Web Design System support for external users and authorized representatives, IEG scripting guidance, new financial hook points, and AppResource docs. It also covers rebranding to Cúram by Merative, accessibility improvements, better dropdown/search and sortable columns, Person Evidence Management, new JMX batch stats properties, pre-authorization sharing, OSS updates, context panel refresh guidance, Microsoft Azure SSO, AKS dev/test support, and removed deprecated features.

children.title: 2 Release notes
children.summary: This section briefly states that detailed release notes list what is new, changed, and fixed in each release, and advises reviewing the notes for your version before installation, deployment, configuration, or customization. It also includes legal and informational notices: usage permissions, copyright and trademark statements, warranty disclaimers, export restrictions, sample code terms, privacy policy link, and third-party product references.
